title: Enhanced magnetic vibration damper with mechanical impedance matching
abstract: Magnetic vibration damper comprising three coaxial elements: a first coaxial element (1) with first permanent magnets (11), a second coaxial element (2) with first soft magnets (21) and a third coaxial element with second permanent magnets (31). The first soft magnets (21) are between the first permanent magnets (11) and the second permanent magnets (31) in a radial direction, and the spacing of the second permanent magnets (31) is larger than the spacing of the first permanent magnets (11). The damper further comprises energy conversion means, such as conductive layers (4) or coils (8) to convert the mechanical movement of the magnets into heat or electric current.
claims: Claims Magnetic vibration damper comprising a first coaxial element (1 ), a second coaxial element (2), at least a third coaxial element (3) and energy conversion means configured to dissipate mechanical energy, being the first coaxial element (1 ), the second coaxial element (2) and the at least one third coaxial element (3) mechanically configured to enable relative movements along a common axis (7), characterized in that: - the first coaxial element (1 ) comprises at least a plurality of first permanent magnets (1 1 ) equally spaced along the axis (7) direction; - the second coaxial element (2) comprises at least a plurality of first soft magnets (21 ) equally spaced along the axis (7) direction; - the at least one third coaxial element (3) comprises at least two second permanent magnets (31 ), and - the at least a plurality of first soft magnets (21 ) being located between the at least a plurality of first permanent magnets (1 1 ) and the at least two second permanent magnets (31 ) in a direction perpendicular to the axis (7) direction. Magnetic vibration damper according to claim 1 characterized in that the at least one plurality of first permanent magnets (1 1 ) of the first coaxial element (1 ) and the at least two second permanent magnets (31 ) of the third coaxial element (3) are axially or radially polarized. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of the previous claims characterized in that, within a distance (L) of twice a separation between the at least two second permanent magnets (31 ) of the third coaxial element (3): - the plurality of first permanent magnets (1 1 ) of the first coaxial element (1 ) comprises a first integer number (n^ of magnets; - the plurality of first soft magnets (21 ) of the second coaxial element (2) comprises a second integer number (n 2 ) of magnets; and - the absolute difference between the first integer number (n^ and the second integer number (n 2 ) equals one. 4. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of the previous claims, wherein the energy conversion means comprises a kinetic to electric energy converter (4, 8), a kinetic to thermal energy converter (61 ), and/or a kinetic to elastic energy converter (62, 91 ). 5. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of the previous claims, wherein the energy conversion means comprise an outer conductive element (4) that dissipates eddy currents generated by magnetic flux variations. 6. Magnetic vibration damper according to claim 5, wherein the conductive element (4) further comprises a plurality of fins that enhance dissipation of the eddy currents heat. 7. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of the previous claims, wherein the third coaxial element (3) further comprises at least a second soft magnet (32) between the at least two second permanent magnets (31 ), and two third soft magnets (33) in the extremes of the at least two second permanent magnets (31 ). 8. Magnetic vibration damper according to claim 7, wherein the at least one second soft magnet (32) and the two third soft magnets (33) of the third coaxial element (3) have the same inner diameter as the at least two second permanent magnets (31 ) and a smaller outer diameter as said at least two second permanent magnets (31 ). 9. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of claims 7 and 8, wherein the at least one second soft magnet (32) and the two third soft magnets (33) of the third coaxial element (3) have half the radial thickness of the at least two second permanent magnets (31 ). 10. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of claims 7 to 9, wherein the at least one second soft magnet (32) has double the axial thickness of the two third soft magnets (33) of the third coaxial element (3). 1 1 . Magnetic vibration damper according to any of the previous claims, wherein the first coaxial element (1 ) further comprises at least a plurality of fourth soft magnets (12), being the fourth soft magnets (12) and the first permanent magnets (1 1 ) disposed alternately along the axis (7) direction. 12. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of the previous claims, wherein the third coaxial element (3) comprises at least two second permanent magnets (31 ) with a smaller diameter than the diameter of the at least one plurality of first soft magnets (21 ) of the second coaxial element (2). 13. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein the third coaxial element (3) comprises at least two second permanent magnets (31 ) with a greater diameter than the diameter of the at least one plurality of first soft magnets (21 ) of the second coaxial element (2). 14. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein the damper comprises a first third coaxial element (3) with a greater diameter than the diameter of the at least one plurality of first soft magnets (21 ) of the second coaxial element (2), and a second third coaxial element (3) with a smaller diameter than the diameter of the at least one plurality of first soft magnets (21 ) of the second coaxial element (2). 15. Magnetic vibration damper according to any of the previous claims, wherein the first coaxial element (1 ) comprises multiple pluralities of first permanent magnets (1 1 ) and the second coaxial element (2) comprises multiple pluralities of first soft magnets (21 ), being the multiple pluralities of first permanent magnets (1 1 ) and the multiple pluralities of soft magnets (21 ) disposed alternately along a direction perpendicular to the axis (7) direction.
